findings.title: Potential null dereference in conversation deletion without soft-delete

history.notes: Status synced from Linear (PLP-96): Todo -> accepted
Status synced from Linear (PLP-96): In Review -> fixed_pending_verify
Status synced from Linear (PLP-96): In Progress -> in_progress
Status synced from Linear (PLP-96): In Review -> fixed_pending_verify
Verified by source review: deleteConversation uses soft-delete and guards missing/deleted conversations before participant access.

findings.description: Conversation deletion now checks that the conversation exists and is not already soft-deleted before accessing participant fields, then soft-deletes by setting deletedAt and maps Prisma P2025 races to NotFoundException.
findings.impact: The delete path no longer dereferences a missing conversation and no longer hard-deletes the row; concurrent delete races return a controlled not-found response.

proof_hooks.summary: deleteConversation fetches the conversation, throws NotFoundException when missing/deleted, validates participant membership, and updates deletedAt instead of deleting the row.
The update is wrapped in a try/catch that converts Prisma P2025 into NotFoundException for concurrent missing/already-deleted rows.

suggested_fix.approach: Review the existing case file and apply the documented remediation.
tests_needed: Re-run the relevant LYRA audit agent.

findings.title: Unsafe profile access in follows.service.ts without existence check

history.notes: Status synced from Linear (PLP-97): Todo -> accepted
Status synced from Linear (PLP-97): In Review -> fixed_pending_verify
Status synced from Linear (PLP-97): In Progress -> in_progress
Status synced from Linear (PLP-97): In Review -> fixed_pending_verify
Fix remains incomplete: profile update path can still fail and now duplicates counter increments.
Verification failed: profile updates are still unsafe for missing Profile rows and now duplicate counter increments.

findings.description: The target user existence check exists, but followUser still calls tx.profile.update for both users inside the transaction. If either Profile row is absent, Prisma can still throw before the later updateMany fallback runs. The later updateMany block also duplicates the counter increments when profiles do exist.
findings.impact: Follow creation can still fail for users without Profile rows, and successful follows can inflate profile counters by two instead of one.

proof_hooks.summary: The transaction uses tx.profile.update for follower and following profiles, which requires both Profile rows to exist.
A second updateMany block increments the same counters again after the transaction, so the attempted fallback creates counter drift.

suggested_fix.approach: Review the existing case file and apply the documented remediation.
tests_needed: Re-run the relevant LYRA audit agent.

findings.title: Unsafe property access in message sender mapping (messaging.service.ts:500)

history.notes: Status synced from Linear (PLP-102): In Review -> fixed_pending_verify
Verified by source review: sender is included in the Prisma result and the schema relation is required.

findings.description: The message sender mapping is backed by a Prisma create query that includes sender.profile, and the schema requires Message.senderId with a required sender relation. With that include shape, sender is materialized before the mapping reads sender.id, sender.username, and sender.profile.
findings.impact: The reviewed sendMessage mapping has the sender relation included before response shaping, so the prior missing-include runtime access issue is resolved for this code path.

proof_hooks.summary: message.create includes sender with nested profile before messageWithSender is built.
Prisma schema declares Message.senderId as required and Message.sender as a required User relation with onDelete Cascade.

suggested_fix.approach: Review the existing case file and apply the documented remediation.
tests_needed: Re-run the relevant LYRA audit agent.

findings.title: Unhandled promise rejection in useMessaging hook error callback

history.notes: Status synced from Linear (PLP-103): In Review -> fixed_pending_verify
Verification failed: error callbacks are still invoked without guarding returned promises.

findings.description: useMessaging still invokes onError and onErrorRef.current directly from socket handlers and catch blocks. Because async functions are assignable to a void callback type in React/TypeScript, a rejecting async onError callback can still produce an unhandled promise rejection.
findings.impact: Consumer-provided async error handlers can still reject outside the hook control flow, causing noisy unhandled rejections and potentially bypassing user-facing error recovery.

proof_hooks.summary: UseMessagingOptions declares onError as a void callback, but async callbacks can still be passed by consumers.
Socket error handlers and catch blocks call onError/onErrorRef.current without Promise.resolve(...).catch handling.

suggested_fix.approach: Review the existing case file and apply the documented remediation.
tests_needed: Re-run the relevant LYRA audit agent.

findings.title: Tool endpoints use one shared workspace for all authenticated users

suggested_fix.approach: Derive workspace_root from user_id under a base path via WorkspacePolicy.workspace_root_for(user_id)
tests_needed: Two users with different JWTs cannot read each other's workspace files

history.notes: Imported from LYRA audit suite finding f-a50b7176 (PLP-562).
Implemented per-user workspace isolation via WorkspacePolicy. All 20 isolation tests pass.
Verified by source review: all tool file operations are scoped by authenticated user_id.

findings.description: Tool endpoints now derive workspace paths from the authenticated JWT subject. WorkspacePolicy creates base_workspace_dir/user_id sandboxes, ToolService requires user_id on public file operations, and routes pass current_user.user_id from the auth dependency.
findings.impact: Authenticated users no longer share one filesystem workspace through these tool endpoints; traversal checks also prevent paths from escaping the per-user sandbox.

proof_hooks.summary: WorkspacePolicy.workspace_root_for(user_id) creates and validates a per-user root under TOOL_WORKSPACE_BASE.
ToolService public methods require user_id and resolve paths through WorkspacePolicy.safe_path.
Tool routes require CurrentUser and pass current_user.user_id into ToolService for list/read/write/delete operations.
get_current_user validates Bearer JWT and rejects missing sub before constructing AuthenticatedUser.

findings.title: Follow creation increments profile counters twice
findings.description: FollowsService.followUser increments follower/following profile counters inside the transaction, then immediately performs a second updateMany increment after the transaction. A successful follow can therefore add 2 to each denormalized counter for a single Follow row.
findings.impact: Follower/following counts drift upward and user profiles show inflated social proof; any ranking or recommendations based on these counters become unreliable.

proof_hooks.summary: Transaction increments both profile counters before returning the created Follow row.
A second updateMany block increments the same follower/following counters again.

suggested_fix.approach: Remove the post-transaction updateMany block or replace the transaction updates with updateMany, but keep exactly one counter increment per successful Follow create.
tests_needed: Add or run a follow/unfollow service test that asserts counters change by exactly one.

history.notes: Created during LYRA audit pass.

findings.title: Music phase-2 vertical is exposed in production UI without a feature flag
findings.description: The Music vertical is now controlled by NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_MUSIC. When disabled, the app shell, onboarding discovery, composer music button/modal, post music cards, about/discovery/earnings copy, and every /music page are gated; direct /music navigation returns a 404 through getServerSideProps.
findings.impact: Production builds keep the incomplete Music phase-2 vertical out of visible UI and direct routes unless the explicit feature flag is enabled.

proof_hooks.summary: Feature flag helper enables Music only for NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_MUSIC=true or 1.
App shell navigation and onboarding omit Music when the flag is disabled.
Music pages export a server-side gate that returns notFound when Music is disabled.
Manual browser test showed no Music nav link on /about and direct /music navigation displayed the 404 page.
Web build passed with Music disabled and again with NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_MUSIC=true.
proof_hooks.command: NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:3003/api NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL=http://localhost:3003 npm --prefix apps/web run build; NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_MUSIC=true NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:3003/api NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL=http://localhost:3003 npm --prefix apps/web run build

suggested_fix.approach: Gate all Music routes and navigation entries behind a production-safe feature flag until the vertical is explicitly ready, or document backend completeness and update the expectation.
tests_needed: Build web with the flag disabled and verify /music is not reachable from navigation/onboarding.

history.notes: Created during LYRA audit pass.
Verified fixed with source gates, disabled-route browser demo, and disabled/enabled web builds.

findings.title: CI and deploy web builds omit required NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL
findings.description: CI and deploy web build jobs now provide both required public web environment variables. CI uses local placeholders for NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL; deploy reads NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL from GitHub environment variables, matching the web build-time validation contract.
findings.impact: Clean CI/deploy web builds no longer fail because NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL is absent from the build environment.

proof_hooks.summary: CI Build Web step sets NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL before npm --prefix apps/web run build.
Deploy Build Web step passes NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL from GitHub environment variables.
Web typecheck and build passed with NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL set while music was disabled.
proof_hooks.command: NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:3003/api NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL=http://localhost:3003 npx tsc --noEmit --project apps/web/tsconfig.json && NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:3003/api NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL=http://localhost:3003 npm --prefix apps/web run build

suggested_fix.approach: Provide NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL in CI/deploy build environments, preferably via repository/environment variables or explicit workflow env entries.
tests_needed: Run next build in a clean env with only the workflow-provided variables.

history.notes: Created during LYRA audit pass.
Verified fixed after workflow env updates and successful web typecheck/build.

regression_checklist: Run npm --prefix apps/web run build in a clean CI-like environment with NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL set.

With production flags disabled, verify Music routes and onboarding links are not exposed.
Run auth endpoint checks for login throttling and reset-token contents.

Critical expectation violation around Music phase-2 exposure.
Build-time env validation conflicts with workflow-provided env.
Remaining auth rate-limit and reset-token findings need targeted verification.
